contracts.name: ProjectStatus
contracts.description: Return contract of the read-only query_project_status MCP tool (the 16th tool). Describes the aggregate APT autonomous-loop status recomputed by status/aggregate.ts. Fields: phase (idle/brainstorming/spec_pending_approval/planning/implementing/verifying/done/blocked), loopDone (true only when all six spec §5 conditions hold, incl. verify PASS), nextAction (single recommended slash-command action per spec §3.3), plus goal/activeSpec/activePlan/specRisk/specApproval/tasks/lastVerify/blockers/summary. Consumed by /current-status, /apt-goal's per-turn decision, and apt-status --json.

contracts.name: StatusSnapshot
contracts.description: Shape persisted to .apt/status.json by status/aggregate.ts writeStatusSnapshot/handleQueryProjectStatus. The recomputed ProjectStatus is the source of truth; this snapshot is a cache with updatedAt. Fields: phase, loopDone, nextAction, updatedAt (ISO). Committed to Git (spec §8); .apt/orchestration/ stays gitignored.

contracts.name: SpecRisk
contracts.description: Spec risk classification (spec §6.2) for a brainstorming spec. "low" = auto-approvable spec (no high-risk trigger); "high" = requires human approval. High-risk triggers: mcp-server changes, new external contract, arch pipeline edits, changedFilesEstimate > 8, or manual frontmatter `risk: high`. Computed by status/risk.ts classifySpecRisk; consumed by status/aggregate.ts to set specApproval (low → auto_approved, high pending → phase=spec_pending_approval). String-literal union alias defined inline in mcp-server/src/status/types.ts.

contracts.name: ApprovalState
contracts.description: Approval state of a spec in the autonomous loop. "pending" = awaiting human approval (high-risk spec); "approved" = human-approved; "auto_approved" = low-risk spec auto-approved by /auto-brainstorm per spec §6.2. Persisted to .apt/approvals.json; consumed by status/aggregate.ts — a high-risk spec in pending state drives phase=spec_pending_approval (loop halts for human sign-off). String-literal union alias defined inline in mcp-server/src/status/types.ts.

contracts.name: VerifyResult
contracts.description: Outcome of a /verify run, parsed from `.apt/verify/latest.md` by status/verify-parse.ts parseVerifyReport/readLatestVerify. "PASS" | "FAIL" | "BLOCKED" | "none" (missing/parseable-less report). Critical to loopDone (spec §5.3): the loop can only complete when lastVerify.result === "PASS". A BLOCKED result (e.g. missing last-scan.json) maps nextAction=start_init. String-literal union alias defined inline in mcp-server/src/status/types.ts.

contracts.name: ApiClientContract
contracts.description: Frontend API-client contract: one card per file using axios/request wrapper. Fields: name, file, description, endpoints[] ({ method: GET|POST|PUT|DELETE|PATCH; path }). Extracted by frontend-api.ts extractApiClients. Populates FrontendPackage.apiClients.

contracts.name: RouteEntry
contracts.description: Frontend route entry (vue-router / React <Route>). Fields: path, name?, component?, meta? (Record<string,unknown>), children? (flattened by extractor with parent+child path concatenation). Extracted by frontend-router.ts extractRoutes. Populates FrontendPackage.routes.

contracts.name: StoreContract
contracts.description: Frontend store contract (Pinia defineStore / Vuex Store / createStore). Fields: name, storeId?, file, description, state[], getters[], actions[]. Mutations fold into actions for Vuex. Extracted by frontend-store.ts extractStores. Populates FrontendPackage.stores.

contracts.name: ProjectOntology
contracts.description: Return contract of the read-only query_ontology(topic?) MCP tool when called without a topic. Describes the full project ontology snapshot recomposed by ontology-query.ts (spec section 3.2) across the loop status, progress, backend modules, frontend packages, registered contracts, and design-system coverage. Fields: project (ProjectMeta|null), status (OntologyStatus: phase/loopDone/nextAction/activeGoal?), progress? (OntologyProgress: currentTask?/doneCount/totalCount), modules[] (ModuleOntology: slug/name/assetCounts), packages[] (PackageOntology: slug/name/framework?/assetCounts), contracts[] (OntologyContract: name/tsFile), design? (OntologyDesign: hasTokens/hasBindings/pages/components), approvalState? (OntologyApprovalState: specRisk?/state?). Consumed by the no-arg query_ontology MCP tool via /auto-brainstorm.

contracts.name: OntologyTopicResult
contracts.description: Return contract of the read-only query_ontology(topic) MCP tool when called with a topic. Describes the focused topic-lookup result produced by ontology-query.ts, scanning module/package names, contract names, and design pages for matches. Fields: topic (the queried string), matchedIn[] (scopes that matched), assets[] (SearchHit from semantic search), contracts[] (OntologyContract hits), designPages? (matching design page ids). Consumed by the query_ontology(topic) MCP tool via /auto-brainstorm.

contracts.name: ProjectMeta
contracts.description: Return contract of the manually-declared project metadata surfaced in the ontology snapshot. Fields: name? (project display name), techStack?[] (declared technology-stack tags). Authored in arch.config.json under projectMeta and consumed by ontology-query.ts and the query_ontology MCP tool.

contracts.name: EntityGraph
contracts.description: Entity graph extracted from JPA annotations, MyBatis XML mappers, and SQL DDL. Contains EntityDef[] (entities with fields/table/source) and EntityRelation[] (one-to-many, many-to-one, etc). Produced by mergeEntityGraphs from three scanners, persisted to .ai/arch/entities.json, surfaced in query_ontology.relations.

contracts.name: FlowGraph
contracts.description: Data flow graph across backend/frontend layers. Contains FlowNode[] (entity/repository/service/controller/api-client/route/store) and FlowEdge[] (directed edges with confidence high/low). Produced by deriveFlowGraph via batch regex cross-layer type-name matching, persisted to .ai/arch/flow.json, queried by query_impact MCP tool.

contracts.name: handleQueryImpact
contracts.description: HandleQueryImpact MCP tool (v2.0.3 18th tool): queries what layers reference a given entity. Reads flow.json and entities.json, groups connected FlowNodes by layer (entity→repository→service→controller→api-client→route→store), sorts high-confidence before low. Three-level degradation: missing flow.json, corrupt JSON, entity not found — each returns note field. Never returns isError.

contracts.name: handleQueryOntology
contracts.description: Core handler for the query_ontology MCP tool (v2.0.2 17th tool, updated v2.0.3 relations, updated v2.0.4 topology+drill). Two modes: (1) no-arg snapshot returns ProjectOntology (project, status, progress?, modules, packages, contracts, design?, approvalState?, relations?, topology?); v2.0.4 adds topology field with moduleCount/rpcEndpoints/entityCount/flowEdgeCount/crossServiceRefs aggregated from entities.json/flow.json; (2) topic focus returns OntologyTopicResult (assets via search_arch, contracts by name match, designPages by slug match); v2.0.4 adds entities? and flowSummary? when topic matches a moduleSlug. All subfields behind try/catch, fault-tolerant.

contracts.name: OntologyTopology
contracts.description: Topology summary for the ontology snapshot (v2.0.4). Fields: moduleCount, rpcEndpoints, entityCount, flowEdgeCount, crossServiceRefs. Pure JSON aggregation from entities.json/flow.json/arch-index. O(1) computation, no vector/LLM dependency. Each count is 0 when source file is missing (0 is valid). Entire field omitted only on total computation failure. Surfaced in query_ontology().topology.

contracts.name: ScannerPlugin
contracts.description: Scanner plugin registry interface (v2.0.4). ScannerPlugin{name, phase:"entity"|"flow"|"asset", scan(ctx)->ScannerResult}. createScannerRegistry() returns 4 plugins: entity-jpa, entity-mybatis, entity-sql (entity phase), flow-derive (flow phase). Pipeline.ts uses registry traversal instead of hardcoded scanner calls, enabling future multi-language plugins in 2.1.

contracts.name: scanJpaEntitiesAst
contracts.description: Java AST entity scanner using java-parser CST (v2.0.4). scanJpaEntitiesAst(projectRoot, modules) -> {entities: EntityDef[], relations: EntityRelation[]}. Parses each @Entity Java file via java-parser Chevrotain CST visitor, precisely extracting @Table name, @Column(name/nullable), @OneToMany(mappedBy) and other relation annotations, generic field types (List<OrderItem>). Per-file try/catch falls back to regex for unparseable files. Called by entity-jpa.ts dispatcher as the main path; entity-jpa-regex.ts is the fallback.

contracts.name: createScannerRegistry
contracts.description: Factory for the scanner plugin registry (v2.0.4). Returns ScannerPlugin[] with 4 plugins across 2 phases: entity phase (entity-jpa via AST dispatcher, entity-mybatis, entity-sql) and flow phase (flow-derive via deriveFlowGraph with RPC Step 4). Entity merge (mergeEntityGraphs) happens in pipeline after entity phase, not as a plugin. Registry traversal replaces hardcoded scanner calls in pipeline.ts runStartInit.
